name: Della Robbia Pound Cake - Country Living
recipeIngredient: 1½ tsp finely grated peeled fresh gingerroot
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 325°F. Grease and flour a 10-inch ring mold or tube pan.
2. In a large bowl, beat butter and sugar until light and fluffy with an electric mixer on medium speed. Add eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition. Beat in vanilla and gingerroot until well blended.
3. Combine flour, baking powder, and salt in a small bowl. Mix into the butter mixture on low speed with an electric mixer. Spread batter evenly in the prepared pan and bake for 30 to 35 minutes or until a tester inserted in the center comes out clean. (While cake bakes, prepare fruit garnish.)
4. Cool the cake in the pan on a wire rack for 10 minutes, then invert the pan onto a wire rack and unmold the cake. Sprinkle rum over the cake if desired; cool completely.
5. Prepare Fruit Garnish: In a 3-quart saucepan, combine cranberry juice, ½ cup sugar, port or red wine, and lemon quarters. Heat to boiling over low heat; simmer, stirring occasionally while preparing fruit.
- Peel one pear and cut it in half vertically; remove the core from both halves. Place one pear half in the simmering juice mixture. After 5 minutes, cut the remaining pear half into wedges about ¼-inch thick and add them to the juice mixture. Simmer for 2 more minutes or until the wedges are barely tender.
- Remove from heat and let the pear half and wedges stand in the juice mixture for 1 hour or until they are rose-colored.
6. In a small saucepan, combine water, remaining ½ cup sugar, and lemon quarters; heat to boiling, stirring occasionally. Avoiding the core, cut the unpeeled remaining pear into ¼-inch-thick wedges. Drop the wedges into the simmering sugar mixture and cook for 2 to 3 minutes or until just tender.
- Remove with a slotted spoon and set aside to drain on paper towels.
7. Heat the sugar mixture over high heat until it reduces to a heavy syrup about 12 minutes. Reduce heat to low and add orange-peel strips; cook until soft-about 5 minutes. With a fork, remove the strips to a small dish and let them cool completely.
8. To decorate the cake, drain the rose-colored pear half and wedges on paper towel. Cut the pear half into ¼-inch-thick wedges for slices that are white inside with a thin rose-colored band around the edge. In a microwave or small saucepan over low heat, melt apple jelly. Brush the top of the cake with some jelly.
- Arrange all three kinds of pear slices in a leaf-like garnish on top of the cake. Brush slices with remaining jelly. Arrange small clusters of grapes among the pear slices and scatter orange-peel strips over the top. Serve or cover loosely with plastic wrap and refrigerate until serving.
